questions.stem:
—______ you ______ your homework yet?
—Yes, I ______ it an hour ago.
questions.explanation: 【解析】yet是现在完成时的标志词，常用于疑问句和否定句，表示"已经"，所以第一空用Have...finished；an hour ago是一般过去时的标志词，表示具体的过去时间，所以第二空用finished。选项A和D的第一空是一般过去时，与yet不搭配；选项C的第二空用了现在完成时，与an hour ago矛盾。【口诀】yet现完，ago过去。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The book ______ I borrowed from the library is very interesting.
questions.explanation: 【解析】本题考查定语从句关系代词的选择。先行词是the book，指物，在从句中作宾语，所以用which或that。选项A的who用于指人，作主语；选项C的whom用于指人，作宾语；选项D的whose表示"谁的"，在从句中作定语。【技巧】先行词是物，作主宾用which/that；先行词是人，作主语用who，作宾语用whom/who/that。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: These computers ______ to the poor students in the village last month.
questions.explanation: 【解析】last month是一般过去时的标志词，主语computers与give之间是被动关系（电脑被送给学生），所以用一般过去时的被动语态were given。选项A是一般现在时被动语态，时态不符；选项C和D是主动语态，且主语computers不能执行"给予"这个动作。【口诀】被动语态be+done，时态体现在be动词上。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem:
—Do you think ______ we will have a picnic tomorrow?
—I'm not sure. It ______ rain in the afternoon.
questions.explanation: 【解析】第一空考查宾语从句连接词。根据答句"I'm not sure"（我不确定）可知，问句表达的是"是否"会去野餐，所以用whether或if。第二空考查情态动词表推测。must表示肯定推测（一定），might表示可能性较小的推测（可能），should表示应该。根据"I'm not sure"可知是不确定，用might最恰当。【注意】虽然if也可以表示"是否"，但在正式英语中，与or not连用或放在句首时多用whether，且此题选项B搭配最合理。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: If you are not ______ with your current score, you should work harder to get a better one.
questions.explanation: 【解析】考查形容词词义辨析。be satisfied with是固定搭配，意为"对……满意"。句意：如果你对自己目前的分数不满意，你应该更努力以取得更好的成绩。选项A strict（严格的）常与with搭配，但意思不符；选项C popular（受欢迎的）常与with/among搭配；选项D nervous（紧张的）常与about搭配。【近义词】content也表示"满意的"，用法为be content with。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: The news made everyone excited, but I felt rather ______.
questions.explanation: 【解析】考查前缀和形容词辨析。根据but可知前后是转折关系，前面说大家都很兴奋，后面应该表示"不感兴趣"。uninterested表示"不感兴趣的"，指对某事缺乏兴趣；disinterested表示"公正的，无私的"，指没有偏见。此处指对消息不感兴趣，用uninterested。前缀un-表示否定，interested表示"感兴趣的"。选项A和D与句意相反。【辨析】uninterested=not interested（不感兴趣）；disinterested=neutral（公正无私）。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: We need to ______ a good plan to solve this problem.
questions.explanation: 【解析】考查动词短语辨析。come up with意为"提出，想出（主意、计划等）"；catch up with意为"赶上（某人）"；keep up with意为"跟上，保持同步"；put up with意为"忍受，容忍"。句意：我们需要想出一个好计划来解决这个问题。根据宾语a good plan可知，应选"提出"。【记忆技巧】come up with = think of（想到）。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a new project called "Green Campus". The goal was to make students ______ the importance of protecting the environment.

As members of the Environmental Club, my classmates and I decided to take action. First, we placed special bins in every classroom for different types of waste. Many students were not ______ to it at first. They often put rubbish into the wrong bins. Instead of getting angry, we stood there ______ and guided them. Slowly, things began to change.

One of the most successful activities was the "Paper Saving Week". We encouraged students to use both sides of paper. To our ______, the amount of waste paper dropped by 40% that week. The teachers were proud of our efforts.

However, the project was not without challenges. Some students complained that separating waste took too much time. We organized a discussion and listened to their ideas. In the end, we found a better way to make the process easier.

This project has taught us that small actions can make a big ______. Protecting the environment is not just a slogan, but something we should practice every day.
questions.explanation: 【解析】考查语境理解。根据上下文，学校开展"绿色校园"项目的目的是让学生"理解"保护环境的重要性。understand（理解）符合语境。forget（忘记）和doubt（怀疑）与项目目的相反；remember（记住）虽然语义通顺，但understand更能体现对重要性的深刻认识，且与后文的taught us相呼应。【线索】后文提到This project has taught us...，说明是让学生理解/明白道理。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a new project called "Green Campus". The goal was to make students ______ the importance of protecting the environment.

As members of the Environmental Club, my classmates and I decided to take action. First, we placed special bins in every classroom for different types of waste. Many students were not ______ to it at first. They often put rubbish into the wrong bins. Instead of getting angry, we stood there ______ and guided them. Slowly, things began to change.

One of the most successful activities was the "Paper Saving Week". We encouraged students to use both sides of paper. To our ______, the amount of waste paper dropped by 40% that week. The teachers were proud of our efforts.

However, the project was not without challenges. Some students complained that separating waste took too much time. We organized a discussion and listened to their ideas. In the end, we found a better way to make the process easier.

This project has taught us that small actions can make a big ______. Protecting the environment is not just a slogan, but something we should practice every day.
questions.explanation: 【解析】考查固定搭配。be used to sth/doing sth是固定搭配，意为"习惯于……"。句意：起初许多学生不习惯（这种分类方式）。根据后文"They often put rubbish into the wrong bins"（他们经常把垃圾放错垃圾桶）可知，学生们还不习惯这种新的分类方式。be kind to（对……友好）；be helpful to（对……有帮助）；be similar to（与……相似）均不符合语境。【口诀】used to do（过去常常），be used to doing（习惯于），be used to do（被用来做）。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a new project called "Green Campus". The goal was to make students ______ the importance of protecting the environment.

As members of the Environmental Club, my classmates and I decided to take action. First, we placed special bins in every classroom for different types of waste. Many students were not ______ to it at first. They often put rubbish into the wrong bins. Instead of getting angry, we stood there ______ and guided them. Slowly, things began to change.

One of the most successful activities was the "Paper Saving Week". We encouraged students to use both sides of paper. To our ______, the amount of waste paper dropped by 40% that week. The teachers were proud of our efforts.

However, the project was not without challenges. Some students complained that separating waste took too much time. We organized a discussion and listened to their ideas. In the end, we found a better way to make the process easier.

This project has taught us that small actions can make a big ______. Protecting the environment is not just a slogan, but something we should practice every day.
questions.explanation: 【解析】考查语境和情感线索。根据"Instead of getting angry"（没有生气）和"guided them"（指导他们）可知，我们应该是有耐心地向他们解释。patiently（耐心地）符合语境。quickly（快速地）与语境不符；secretly（秘密地）和suddenly（突然地）不符合逻辑。【线索】Instead of getting angry提示了对比关系，没有生气→有耐心。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a new project called "Green Campus". The goal was to make students ______ the importance of protecting the environment.

As members of the Environmental Club, my classmates and I decided to take action. First, we placed special bins in every classroom for different types of waste. Many students were not ______ to it at first. They often put rubbish into the wrong bins. Instead of getting angry, we stood there ______ and guided them. Slowly, things began to change.

One of the most successful activities was the "Paper Saving Week". We encouraged students to use both sides of paper. To our ______, the amount of waste paper dropped by 40% that week. The teachers were proud of our efforts.

However, the project was not without challenges. Some students complained that separating waste took too much time. We organized a discussion and listened to their ideas. In the end, we found a better way to make the process easier.

This project has taught us that small actions can make a big ______. Protecting the environment is not just a slogan, but something we should practice every day.
questions.explanation: 【解析】考查固定搭配和语境。to one's surprise是固定短语，意为"令某人惊讶的是"。根据"dropped by 40%"（下降了40%）可知，这个结果令人惊讶。sadness（悲伤）；fear（恐惧）；regret（后悔）均不符合语境。【固定搭配】to one's joy/delight（令某人高兴的是）；to one's disappointment（令某人失望的是）。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a new project called "Green Campus". The goal was to make students ______ the importance of protecting the environment.

As members of the Environmental Club, my classmates and I decided to take action. First, we placed special bins in every classroom for different types of waste. Many students were not ______ to it at first. They often put rubbish into the wrong bins. Instead of getting angry, we stood there ______ and guided them. Slowly, things began to change.

One of the most successful activities was the "Paper Saving Week". We encouraged students to use both sides of paper. To our ______, the amount of waste paper dropped by 40% that week. The teachers were proud of our efforts.

However, the project was not without challenges. Some students complained that separating waste took too much time. We organized a discussion and listened to their ideas. In the end, we found a better way to make the process easier.

This project has taught us that small actions can make a big ______. Protecting the environment is not just a slogan, but something we should practice every day.
questions.explanation: 【解析】考查固定搭配。make a difference是固定短语，意为"产生影响，发挥作用"。句意：这个项目教会我们，小小的行动可以产生巨大的影响。make a decision（做决定）；make a mistake（犯错误）；make a plan（制定计划）虽然都是固定搭配，但不符合句意。【金句】Small actions can make a big difference.（积少成多/小行动大影响。）
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
In today's digital age, more and more teenagers are suffering from eye problems. Dr. Li Ming, a famous eye doctor from Shenzhen Hospital, has proposed a simple but effective method called the "20-20-20" rule to help protect our eyes.

According to Dr. Li, when we look at screens for a long time, our eye muscles become tense. This tension, or "strain", can cause headaches and blurry vision. The 20-20-20 rule is easy to follow: every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet (about 6 meters) away for at least 20 seconds. This gives your eye muscles a chance to relax.

"Many students think wearing glasses is the only solution to eye problems," says Dr. Li. "But actually, changing our habits is more important. I often tell my patients that prevention is better than cure."

A recent study shows that students who follow this rule regularly have 30% less eye strain than those who don't. However, Dr. Li reminds us that the rule alone is not enough. We should also make sure to spend at least one hour outdoors every day, as natural light is good for eye development.

Schools in Shenzhen have started to promote this rule. Some teachers even set timers in class to remind students to rest their eyes. Students who have tried the method say their eyes feel more comfortable after a day of studying.
questions.stem: According to the passage, what does the "20-20-20" rule suggest we do?
options: A. Study for 20 minutes and rest for 20 seconds
B. Look at something 20 feet away every 20 minutes for 20 seconds
C. Spend 20 minutes outdoors every day
D. Check our eyes every 20 days
questions.explanation: 【解析】细节理解题。根据第二段"every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet (about 6 meters) away for at least 20 seconds"可知，20-20-20规则指的是：每20分钟，看20英尺（约6米）外的东西至少20秒。选项A错误，不是学习20分钟休息20秒；选项C错误，每天户外活动一小时是额外建议，不是20-20-20规则的内容；选项D文中未提及。【定位技巧】根据题干关键词"20-20-20 rule"定位到第二段，找到冒号后的解释说明。
questions.label: 阅读-细节理解

questions.group_passage:
In today's digital age, more and more teenagers are suffering from eye problems. Dr. Li Ming, a famous eye doctor from Shenzhen Hospital, has proposed a simple but effective method called the "20-20-20" rule to help protect our eyes.

According to Dr. Li, when we look at screens for a long time, our eye muscles become tense. This tension, or "strain", can cause headaches and blurry vision. The 20-20-20 rule is easy to follow: every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet (about 6 meters) away for at least 20 seconds. This gives your eye muscles a chance to relax.

"Many students think wearing glasses is the only solution to eye problems," says Dr. Li. "But actually, changing our habits is more important. I often tell my patients that prevention is better than cure."

A recent study shows that students who follow this rule regularly have 30% less eye strain than those who don't. However, Dr. Li reminds us that the rule alone is not enough. We should also make sure to spend at least one hour outdoors every day, as natural light is good for eye development.

Schools in Shenzhen have started to promote this rule. Some teachers even set timers in class to remind students to rest their eyes. Students who have tried the method say their eyes feel more comfortable after a day of studying.
questions.stem: What can we infer from Dr. Li Ming's words in Paragraph 3?
options: A. All students should wear glasses to protect eyes
B. Eye problems cannot be cured once they occur
C. Changing habits is important for eye health
D. Wearing glasses is better than preventing eye problems
questions.explanation: 【解析】推理判断题。根据第三段Dr. Li的话"Many students think wearing glasses is the only solution... But actually, changing our habits is more important"可知，他认为改变习惯比戴眼镜更重要，预防胜于治疗。由此可推断出改变习惯对眼睛健康很重要。选项A与原文相反；选项B过于绝对，文中未说不能治愈；选项D与原文"prevention is better than cure"（预防胜于治疗）相反。【推理技巧】注意转折词But，其后往往是作者真实观点。
questions.label: 阅读-推理判断

questions.group_passage:
In today's digital age, more and more teenagers are suffering from eye problems. Dr. Li Ming, a famous eye doctor from Shenzhen Hospital, has proposed a simple but effective method called the "20-20-20" rule to help protect our eyes.

According to Dr. Li, when we look at screens for a long time, our eye muscles become tense. This tension, or "strain", can cause headaches and blurry vision. The 20-20-20 rule is easy to follow: every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet (about 6 meters) away for at least 20 seconds. This gives your eye muscles a chance to relax.

"Many students think wearing glasses is the only solution to eye problems," says Dr. Li. "But actually, changing our habits is more important. I often tell my patients that prevention is better than cure."

A recent study shows that students who follow this rule regularly have 30% less eye strain than those who don't. However, Dr. Li reminds us that the rule alone is not enough. We should also make sure to spend at least one hour outdoors every day, as natural light is good for eye development.

Schools in Shenzhen have started to promote this rule. Some teachers even set timers in class to remind students to rest their eyes. Students who have tried the method say their eyes feel more comfortable after a day of studying.
questions.stem: What does the word "strain" in Paragraph 2 probably mean?
questions.explanation: 【解析】词义猜测题。根据上下文，"This tension, or 'strain'"中or表示同位语解释，说明strain与tension（紧张）意思相近。前文说"when we look at screens for a long time, our eye muscles become tense"（长时间看屏幕，眼部肌肉变得紧张），后文说会导致头痛和视力模糊。因此strain指的是眼部肌肉的"疲劳；紧张"。选项A、C、D均不符合语境。【猜词技巧】利用同位语（tension, or strain）、因果关系（cause headaches）和上下文逻辑进行猜测。
questions.label: 阅读-词义猜测

questions.group_passage:
In today's digital age, more and more teenagers are suffering from eye problems. Dr. Li Ming, a famous eye doctor from Shenzhen Hospital, has proposed a simple but effective method called the "20-20-20" rule to help protect our eyes.

According to Dr. Li, when we look at screens for a long time, our eye muscles become tense. This tension, or "strain", can cause headaches and blurry vision. The 20-20-20 rule is easy to follow: every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet (about 6 meters) away for at least 20 seconds. This gives your eye muscles a chance to relax.

"Many students think wearing glasses is the only solution to eye problems," says Dr. Li. "But actually, changing our habits is more important. I often tell my patients that prevention is better than cure."

A recent study shows that students who follow this rule regularly have 30% less eye strain than those who don't. However, Dr. Li reminds us that the rule alone is not enough. We should also make sure to spend at least one hour outdoors every day, as natural light is good for eye development.

Schools in Shenzhen have started to promote this rule. Some teachers even set timers in class to remind students to rest their eyes. Students who have tried the method say their eyes feel more comfortable after a day of studying.
questions.stem: What is the best title for this passage?
options: B. The Importance of Outdoor Activities
C. A Simple Rule for Eye Protection
questions.explanation: 【解析】主旨大意题。文章主要介绍了Dr. Li Ming提出的20-20-20规则，用于保护眼睛健康。第一段引出话题，第二段详细解释规则内容，第三段强调改变习惯的重要性，第四、五段补充说明和推广情况。因此"A Simple Rule for Eye Protection"（一个简单的护眼规则）最能概括全文。选项A只是文中提到的细节；选项B是补充建议，非主要内容；选项D范围过大，文章只聚焦于眼睛健康。【主旨题技巧】寻找反复出现的关键词（20-20-20 rule, eye protection），标题要概括全文而非局部细节。
questions.label: 阅读-主旨大意

questions.group_passage:
Good time management is important for middle school students. (17)______ Here are some practical ways to improve your time management skills.

Start by tracking your time. (18)______ Write down everything you do for a week. You might be surprised to find how much time you spend on your phone.

Next, plan ahead. Make a to-do list every morning. (19)______ Put the most important tasks at the top. This way, you won't forget anything urgent.

(20)______ Don't try to do everything perfectly. Focus on what really matters, and you will find your school life much easier.
options: A. However, don't be too hard on yourself.
B. First, find out where your time actually goes.
C. Many students struggle to balance study and play.
D. Then, decide which tasks are most important.
E. But remember to leave some time for relaxation.
questions.explanation: 【解析】选句子填空题。空格前说时间管理很重要，空格后说"这里有一些实用的方法来提高你的时间管理技能"。选项C"Many students struggle to balance study and play"（许多学生努力平衡学习和娱乐）承上启下，先指出问题，再引出解决方法。其他选项：A用于最后一段的转折；B与下一段的Start by tracking对应；D与下文的Put the most important tasks对应；E是干扰项。【技巧】首段末句通常引出下文，找出问题-解决方案的逻辑关系。
questions.label: 短文填空

questions.group_passage:
Good time management is important for middle school students. (17)______ Here are some practical ways to improve your time management skills.

Start by tracking your time. (18)______ Write down everything you do for a week. You might be surprised to find how much time you spend on your phone.

Next, plan ahead. Make a to-do list every morning. (19)______ Put the most important tasks at the top. This way, you won't forget anything urgent.

(20)______ Don't try to do everything perfectly. Focus on what really matters, and you will find your school life much easier.
options: A. However, don't be too hard on yourself.
B. First, find out where your time actually goes.
C. Many students struggle to balance study and play.
D. Then, decide which tasks are most important.
E. But remember to leave some time for relaxation.
questions.explanation: 【解析】考查逻辑顺序。本段主题是"Start by tracking your time"（开始追踪你的时间），选项B"First, find out where your time actually goes"（首先，找出你的时间实际上花在哪里了）与本段主题一致，且First与Start by相呼应。后文的"Write down everything..."是对"find out where your time actually goes"的具体解释。【线索】注意段落主题句Start by...和表示顺序的First。
questions.label: 短文填空

questions.group_passage:
Good time management is important for middle school students. (17)______ Here are some practical ways to improve your time management skills.

Start by tracking your time. (18)______ Write down everything you do for a week. You might be surprised to find how much time you spend on your phone.

Next, plan ahead. Make a to-do list every morning. (19)______ Put the most important tasks at the top. This way, you won't forget anything urgent.

(20)______ Don't try to do everything perfectly. Focus on what really matters, and you will find your school life much easier.
options: A. However, don't be too hard on yourself.
B. First, find out where your time actually goes.
C. Many students struggle to balance study and play.
D. Then, decide which tasks are most important.
E. But remember to leave some time for relaxation.
questions.explanation: 【解析】考查连接词和逻辑关系。前文说"Make a to-do list every morning"（每天早上列待办清单），后文说"Put the most important tasks at the top"（把最重要的任务放在最上面）。选项D"Then, decide which tasks are most important"（然后，决定哪些任务最重要）承上启下，Then表示顺序，承接上文的列清单，引出下文的重要性排序。【线索】Then表示步骤的延续，与Next（下一段开头）形成顺序呼应。
questions.label: 短文填空

questions.group_passage:
Good time management is important for middle school students. (17)______ Here are some practical ways to improve your time management skills.

Start by tracking your time. (18)______ Write down everything you do for a week. You might be surprised to find how much time you spend on your phone.

Next, plan ahead. Make a to-do list every morning. (19)______ Put the most important tasks at the top. This way, you won't forget anything urgent.

(20)______ Don't try to do everything perfectly. Focus on what really matters, and you will find your school life much easier.
options: A. However, don't be too hard on yourself.
B. First, find out where your time actually goes.
C. Many students struggle to balance study and play.
D. Then, decide which tasks are most important.
E. But remember to leave some time for relaxation.
questions.explanation: 【解析】考查转折关系。空格后说"Don't try to do everything perfectly"（不要试图把每件事都做得完美），这是一种让步或转折的建议。选项A"However, don't be too hard on yourself"（然而，不要对自己太苛刻）与后文意思一致，However表示转折，提醒读者虽然要做好时间管理，但也不要过分苛求完美。【线索】However表示转折，通常引出与前面建议相对或补充的限制条件。
questions.label: 短文填空
